meta.panel_summary: The weakest link is the two Results statistics (c1, c2): both are internally impossible as printed - the F-values cannot coexist with their own p-values - and statcheck.v1 flagged each (severity B), with both flags reproducing deterministically. The panel reads this as a benign dropped-decimal typesetting error already covered by the published correction (PMC5703034) rather than a data-integrity problem, since a single mechanical cause explains both and confers no advantage. Overall the paper's central merit-sharing conclusion is not overturned, but the printed numbers are non-recoverable and directionally ambiguous (null vs. significant), so a human should confirm the corrected values against the raw data and note the absence of effect sizes/CIs.

findings.message: reported p-value disagrees with the statistic AND flips the .05 significance decision
findings.discrepancy: reported p=0.405 but F statistic 71 (df1=1,df2=32) admits p in [0,0] — crosses .05

evidence.expected_output: INCONSISTENT test=F stat=71 df1=1,df2=32 reported_p=0.405 achievable_p=[0,0]

findings.message: reported p-value disagrees with the statistic AND flips the .05 significance decision
findings.discrepancy: reported p=0.488 but F statistic 49 (df1=1,df2=32) admits p in [0,0] — crosses .05

evidence.expected_output: INCONSISTENT test=F stat=49 df1=1,df2=32 reported_p=0.488 achievable_p=[0,0]

routed_to_human.note: Methodologist (T3): statistic<->p internally inconsistent; reproducibility failure where which token is corrupt (F or p) is not determinable from the text; ANOVAs reported without effect sizes/CIs. Confirm corrected values against the official correction and raw data.

Claims Auditor (T4): the F and p halves point in opposite evidential directions, so the printed result licenses neither an effect nor a null conclusion; any 'no difference' reading drawn from p~.40-.49 is unverifiable as printed and, if F is truly small, rests on a modest sample (N~34).

Domain Expert: df(1,32)~N=33-34 is field-typical and well-powered; but the decimal repair is ambiguous in direction - reconstructing from magnitude (F~7.1, eta2~.18, significant) vs from the printed p (F~0.71, eta2~.02, null) yields opposite scientific conclusions. Printed p-values imply these terms were null; consult PMC5703034.

Integrity Screener (T7): LOW concern, consistent with an innocent production error. A single dropped-leading-decimal (71->0.71, 49->0.49) reproduces the printed p-values; same uniform error type on both, inflates nothing/gives no author advantage, recoverable to a coherent benign value. No terminal-digit, duplication, or N-inconsistency signals. Treat as the documented production error already addressed by the official correction.
